whyChoose.title: Why Generate Lyric Videos on LyricVideoMaker
whyChoose.description: Most lyric video generators still make you type lyrics and drag text on a timeline. LyricVideoMaker is built for music: it reads your audio and treats the lyrics as the centerpiece, not an afterthought.

highlight.title: The lyric video creator that does the typing for you
highlight.description: Transcription, beat sync, and visuals happen automatically from your audio. You review and adjust — you never build from a blank timeline.

cards.title: Beat-accurate auto-sync
cards.description: Every lyric line is timed to the millisecond using audio analysis — not just speech detection. The AI reads your song's BPM and energy, so lyrics land on the beat, not just near it.

cards.title: Lyrics you can trust (and fix)
cards.description: Transcription runs at 95%+ accuracy across 50+ languages. See every line with its timestamp before generating — click to edit words, merge lines, or adjust timing.

cards.title: Styles built for lyrics, not subtitles
cards.description: These aren't caption presets. Each template animates lyrics as the visual centerpiece — with motion that reacts to your song's tempo and mood.

howItWorks.title: How to Generate a Lyric Video

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drag in an audio file — MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, or OGG — or paste a link. No account needed to start, and no manual lyric typing required.

steps.title: Review your lyrics and pick a style
steps.description: AI transcribes and syncs every line in about 30 seconds. Edit any line you want, then preview templates with your actual lyrics until one fits the song.

steps.title: Preview and export
steps.description: Watch the full lyric video before publishing, then export in HD — 16:9 for YouTube, 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, or 1:1 for feed posts. No subscription required.

items.question: Is this AI lyric video generator free?
items.answer: Yes. Sign up, get free credits, and export your lyric video — no subscription required and no credit card needed. This is a free AI lyric video generator you can use end to end, and one of the best AI lyric video generator options if you want to create lyric video projects from audio without manual timing.

items.question: What's the difference between this and a subtitle generator?
items.answer: Subtitle tools place plain text at the bottom of a video. A lyric video generator makes the lyrics the visual focus — animated, styled, and synced to the music's rhythm, generated directly from your audio.

items.question: Can I generate a lyric video from a YouTube link?
items.answer: Yes. Paste a YouTube or SoundCloud URL and the AI extracts the audio, then transcribes and syncs the lyrics. Make sure you have the rights to use the track.

items.question: Can I change the font, colors, or background?
items.answer: Yes. Every template lets you adjust fonts, color palettes, and backgrounds so the lyric video matches your release — no design tools needed.

items.question: My song has no vocals in the intro — how is that handled?
items.answer: Instrumental sections are detected automatically. You can show a title card, a visualizer animation, or nothing through those stretches — your choice.

items.question: How does the AI generate the visuals?
items.answer: The AI reads the mood, energy, and themes of your song and generates matching visual scenes. Every video feels like it belongs to the track, not a generic template.

items.question: Can I use exported lyric videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes — your exports are yours, including for monetized channels and paid releases. Just make sure you have the rights to the music you upload.

contentSections.heading: What is an AI lyric video generator?
contentSections.intro: An AI lyric video generator turns a song into a finished video where the lyrics are the visual centerpiece — animated, styled, and synced to the music. Instead of typing words into a timeline, you upload the audio and the generator transcribes the lyrics, times each line to the beat, and builds matching visuals automatically.
bullets: Input: an audio file of your song — no video footage and no typing required.
What it does: transcribes the lyrics, aligns each line to the beat, and generates mood-driven visuals.
Output: a finished MP4 lyric video in 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1.
Not a subtitle generator: a subtitle tool only pins plain text to the bottom of an existing clip.

contentSections.heading: What the generator does automatically vs what you control
contentSections.intro: Automatic does not mean locked. The generator handles the slow, repetitive work, and you keep control of the details that make a release feel finished.

rows: Transcribing the lyrics from your audio
Aligning every line to the beat
Nudging the timing of a line
Detecting song structure and instrumental gaps
Showing a title card or visualizer in those gaps
Sizing and placing the lyrics for each format

contentSections.heading: Lyric video styles by genre
contentSections.intro: The generator reads your song's mood, energy, and genre and leans the visual direction to match — so a ballad and a rap hook never get the same look.

columns: Visual direction the AI leans toward
rows: Clean type, generous spacing, gentle motion
Bold type, bright color, beat-reactive movement
Tight, high-contrast layouts with punchy timing
High-energy motion that hits on the drop
Slower pacing, moody color, atmospheric visuals

contentSections.heading: Tips for a lyric video people actually watch
contentSections.intro: A few habits separate a lyric video that holds attention from one viewers swipe past:
bullets: Keep one or two lines on screen at a time — a full verse at once is hard to read on a phone.
Let the chorus look different from the verses so viewers feel the song's structure.
Match the font to the genre: softer serifs suit ballads, bold sans-serif reads better for rap, pop, and dance.
Sync transitions to the beat, not just to the lyric — the video feels more musical.
Keep instrumental sections alive with motion or a visualizer so the screen never goes empty.
Preview on a phone before publishing — if it reads on a small screen, it reads everywhere.
Reuse a consistent style across releases so your lyric videos feel like one artist world.

contentSections.heading: How an AI generator compares to other ways to make a lyric video
contentSections.intro: There are four common ways to make a lyric video. The generator wins on speed and on doing the lyric work for you.

contentSections.heading: The fastest first step for almost any song
body: Because the generator turns the unknowns into something you can see — line breaks, timing, readability, and overall mood — it is the safest first pass even when you plan to polish the video elsewhere. You get a watchable draft in minutes, at almost no cost, and only invest in a heavier production if the song truly needs it.

It is also built for the realities of a release: one upload exports a full-length YouTube version, a vertical hook for short-form, and a square cut for feed posts, all sharing the same lyrics and visual direction. That makes it practical not just for a single video, but for keeping a whole release week supplied with on-brand content.
